title: Sorgame Endralum Lyrics Ooru Vittu ooru Vanthu
stanzas: <div>Sorgame Endralum Song Lyrics Ooru Vittu ooru Vanthu

Sorgame Endraalum
adhu nammoora pola varuma
ada ennaadu endraalum
adhu nam naatuku eedaaguma

pala dhesam muzhuthum
pesum mozhigal
Tamizh pol inithidumaa..

Sorgame Endraalum
adhu nammoora pola varuma
ada ennaadu endraalum
adhu nam naatuku eedaaguma

aerikarai paattum
yaelaelaelo paattum
ingae yedhum ketkavillaiye

paadum kuyil saaththam
aadum mayil niththam
paakka oru solai illaiye

veththalaiya madichu
maaman adha kadichu
thuppa oru vazhi illaiye

oadi vandhu kudhichu
mungi mungi kulichi
aada oru oadai illaiye

ivvooru enna ooru
nammooru romba maelu
ada odum pala caru
enna aadambaram paaru

oru thaagam theerkka
yaedhu moru

Sorgame Endraalum
adhu nammoora pola varuma
ada ennaadu endraalum
adhu nam naatuku eedaaguma

pala dhesam muzhuthum
pesum mozhigal
Tamizh pol inithidumaa..

Sorgame Endraalum
adhu nammoora pola varuma
ada ennaadu endraalum
adhu nam naatuku eedaaguma

maadu kanu maekka
maeyiradha paakka
mandha veli ingu illaiye

aadupuli aattam
poatu vilaiyaada
arasa maram
maedai illaiye

kaala rendu pootti
katta vandi oati
gaanam paada
vazhi illaiye

tholigala alaichi
solli solli rasichi
attam poda mudiyalaiye

oru endhiratha pola
ada ingae ulla vazhka
idha engae poyi solla
manam ishta padavilla

nammoora pola
oorum illa.

Sorgame Endraalum
adhu nammoora pola varuma
ada ennaadu endraalum
adhu nam naatuku eedaaguma

pala dhesam muzhuthum
pesum mozhigal
Tamizh pol inithidumaa..

Sorgame Endraalum
adhu nammoora pola varuma
ada ennaadu endraalum
adhu nam naatuku eedaaguma

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Sorgame Endralum?

The song reflects on the essence of life and the beauty of Tamil culture, emphasizing the connection between people and their roots.

What is the context of the song in the movie Ooru Vittu Ooru Vanthu?

This song plays a significant role in portraying the cultural values and emotional depth of the characters in the film.

Who are the artists behind the song?

The song features the iconic voices of Ilaiyaraaja and S. Janaki, both of whom are celebrated figures in Tamil music.

When was Sorgame Endralum released?

The song was released on July 14, 1990, and has since become a beloved classic.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Sorgame Endralum, the lyrics evoke a deep sense of nostalgia and connection to Tamil culture, making it a poignant melody. The song beautifully conveys themes of unity and the essence of home, resonating with listeners on an emotional level. It serves as a reminder of the cultural richness and the bonds that tie people together. The soothing composition by Ilaiyaraaja, combined with S. Janaki's heartfelt vocals, creates an unforgettable listening experience.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Sorgame Endralum is a timeless melody that resonates with the essence of Tamil culture, beautifully rendered by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja and S. Janaki. This enchanting song from the film Ooru Vittu Ooru Vanthu captures the heart with its soulful lyrics and captivating composition. Experience the nostalgia and devotion that this classic brings, making it a must-listen for every Tamil music lover.
stanzaMeanings: Heaven, will it come to our place? If it comes, will it be good for our country?
Many countries speak languages, will Tamil be one of them?
There are songs everywhere, but no one is listening here.
The singing cuckoo and dancing peacock, there is no garden to see them.
The bitter gourd is eaten, uncle, there is no way to throw it away.
Running and jumping, there is no way to play.
What is this place? Our place is very high, look at the many things happening.
Which hunger can be satisfied?
Heaven, will it come to our place? If it comes, will it be good for our country?
Many countries speak languages, will Tamil be one of them?
Heaven, will it come to our place? If it comes, will it be good for our country?
There is no place to see the cow's eyes.
The dancing tiger plays, but there is no royal tree to climb.
Two times the time is wasted, there is no way to sing a song.
Telling and telling, enjoying, but the dance cannot be done.
Like a machine, this life is not something I want to talk about.
There is no place like our place.
Heaven, will it come to our place? If it comes, will it be good for our country?
Many countries speak languages, will Tamil be one of them?
Heaven, will it come to our place? If it comes, will it be good for our country?
